name: Biscayan Basque (Bizkaiera)

ancestors.notes: Biscayan shares the standard Basque grapheme inventory but diverges significantly in morphology and phonology; inherits grapheme-to-phoneme base from Batua.

notes: Biscayan (Bizkaiera/Bizkaia). Western Basque, spoken in Biscay and Araba. VALIDATED and CORRECTED against Bedialauneta Txurruka & Hualde (2023), an Illustration of the IPA of Markina Basque (a representative Biscayan variety). Key phonological features actually documented there: (1) MERGER of the apico-alveolar /s̺/ and lamino-alveolar /s̻/ fricatives in favour of a single apico-alveolar /s̺/, and of the affricates /ts̺/ and /ts̻/ in favour of a single denti-alveolar /ts̻/ (Bedialauneta & Hualde 2023: 1098) — so orthographic s and z both give /s̺/, and ts and tz both give /ts̻/; the standard three-way sibilant contrast of Batua is NOT present here. (2) MERGER of the voiceless palatal stop /c/ (spelled tt) with the postalveolar affricate /tʃ/, in favour of /tʃ/ (2023: 1098). (3) PALATALISATION after /i/: /s/→[ʃ] (haizea > /aiʃie/), /t/→[tʃ], /n/→[ɲ], /l/→[ʎ] (2023: 1102-1104). (4) NO aspiration: western Basque lost /h/ and has no aspirated stops (Hualde 2018: 2). (5) Voiced stops /b d ɡ/ are approximants [β ð ɣ] intervocalically and nasals assimilate in place to a following consonant (2023: 1099, 1103). Morphologically the most divergent dialect from Batua. NOTE (engine limit): the well-known Biscayan raising of the singular article /-a/ to [-e] after a high vowel (baso+a > basue, kale+a > kalie; Bedialauneta & Hualde 2023: 1107-1108) is a morphophonological process on the article, not a context-free grapheme rule, and is therefore not modelled at the orthography-to-IPA level.

sources.author: Bedialauneta Txurruka, I. & Hualde, J.I.
sources.title: Markina Basque (Illustration of the IPA)
sources.publisher: Journal of the International Phonetic Association 53(3)
sources.notes: Primary read source for Biscayan phonology: sibilant/affricate/palatal-stop mergers (p.1098), palatalisation after /i/ (pp.1102-1104), article vowel raising (pp.1107-1108).

sources.author: Hualde, J.I.
sources.title: Aspiration in Basque
sources.notes: Read for the loss of aspiration in western (Biscayan) Basque (p.84 fn.).

sources.author: Hualde, J.I. & Ortiz de Urbina, J. (eds.)
sources.title: A Grammar of Basque
